data.title: Hackers exploit Ethereum smart contracts to distribute malware, using poisoned NPM packages; Binance connects it to DPRK.
data.description: Malicious actors are utilizing Ethereum smart contracts to distribute malware through tainted NPM packages. Binance has tied this activity to attackers from DPRK, indicating a serious security concern in the industry.

data.title: Giving money to poor families at specific times lowered young children death rates by almost 50%, reveals a recent study.
data.description: A recent study discovered that providing financial assistance to impoverished families during specific periods resulted in a nearly 50% decrease in deaths among young children. The research emphasized the significant impact of monetary support on reducing mortality rates in this vulnerable demographic.

data.title: Bitcoin has outperformed S&P500, Nasdaq, gold, real estate, and 10-year Treasury bonds over the past decade. Bitcoin's 46% real return surpasses its competitors.
data.description: Over the past decade, Bitcoin has shown superior performance compared to other asset classes, despite its notable volatility. In a recent analysis, macro investor Krueger compared the nominal and inflation-adjusted returns of Bitcoin between 2014 and 2024 with those of the S&P 500, Nasdaq, gold, U.S. real estate, and 10-year Treasury bonds. When factoring in 7% "true" inflation and a 20% capital gains tax, Bitcoin exhibited a substantial 46% real return, far surpassing its competitors like Nasdaq with +4% and S&P. This information was shared in a post on X on Sep. 1. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: T-Mobile projects $1.2 billion in cost synergies, shortening integration timeline to 2 years after UScellular acquisition.
data.description: T-Mobile US Inc. (NASDAQ: TMUS) has revised its guidance following the completion of acquiring UScellular. The company has raised its cost-savings target to $1.2 billion annually from the initial forecast of $1 billion. The integration timeline has been shortened to two years from the previous estimate of three to four years. COO Srini Gopalan stated that the merger would improve service for subscribers of both companies. T-Mobile anticipates operational expense reductions of $950 million and capital expense efficiencies of $250 million. The integration is expected to incur certain costs.
